posts.author: NicklasL (Nicklas Lohmann )
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying to make a integration with Make.com, where i update the multi-person column from one item to another, but i cant seem to find the data structure to input the multiple teams and persons into the column
i have looked at the Guide to Changing Column Values
but i cant seem to find the datastructures of the different column types?

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @NicklasL ,

To update a multi-person column in Monday.com using their API, you need to understand the data structure required for the column. The documentation is a good resource, but it might not provide detailed information about specific column types like the multi-person column

Here’s an example of how you might structure the data for updating a multi-person column:

mutation {
change_column_value(board_id: your_board_id, item_id: your_item_id, column_id: “your_column_id”, value: “{"personsAndTeams":[{"kind":"person","id":123456789},{"kind":"person","id":987654321}]}”) {
id
}
}

You can also do this using Make.com

you can easily create automated workflows that connect Monday.com with other apps and services. For example, if you need to update multi-person columns in Monday.com , Make.com
allows you to define the data structure required for the column and automate the process of transferring data between systems

Do you also have a solution for the Timeline Data-structure as in this request?

Timeline data structure monday apps & integrations
Hi, i want to fill in a timeline from Make.com but cant seem to figure out the data structure I have followed this solution: Timeline structure for subitem Integromat And it works if i delete the “Timeline” part but then if i ad it as milestone, the milestoner checker doesn’t work it looks like this [image] when it should look like this [image] I get an error when i send this (i map the visualization_type first) [image]

Or maybe a way for me to figure out the data-format myself

i already make it using Make.com, but because i use dynamic board mapping i couldn’t use the build in columntype mapping.
And therefore need to send the JSON string as you added

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @NicklasL ,

You can try the json like this

{“to”:“2020-10-14”,“from”:“2020-10-14”,“visualization_type”:“milestone”}

image835×820 80.6 KB

It gives me a milestone
